select_perm.solution:
Proof.
  intros x l. generalize dependent x.
  induction l as [| h t IH]; intros; simpl in *.
  - inv H. apply Permutation_refl.
  - bdestruct (x <=? h).
    + destruct (select x t) as [j l'] eqn:E.
      inv H.
      specialize (IH x). rewrite E in IH.
      apply perm_trans with (x :: h :: t).
      * apply Permutation_refl.
      * apply perm_skip.
        apply perm_trans with (j :: l').
        -- apply perm_trans with (x :: t). apply Permutation_refl. apply IH. reflexivity.
        -- constructor.
      Restart.
  intros x l. generalize dependent x.
  induction l as [| h t IH]; intros; simpl in *.
  - inv H. constructor.
  - bdestruct (x <=? h).
    + destruct (select x t) as [j l'] eqn:E. inv H.
      specialize (IH x j l' E).
      eauto.
    + destruct (select h t) as [j l'] eqn:E. inv H.
      specialize (IH h j l' E).
      eauto.
Qed.
select_perm.explanation: By induction on l. Destruct the select result and use the IH with eauto to find the right permutation via perm_trans.

select_rest_length.solution:
Proof.
  intros.
  apply select_perm in H.
  apply Permutation_length in H.
  simpl in H. lia.
Qed.
select_rest_length.explanation: Use select_perm to get a permutation, then Permutation_length to equate lengths.

selsort_perm.solution:
Proof.
  induction n as [| n' IH]; intros.
  - destruct l; simpl. constructor. discriminate.
  - destruct l as [| x r].
    + simpl. constructor.
    + simpl. destruct (select x r) as [y r'] eqn:E.
      apply perm_trans with (y :: r').
      * apply select_perm. assumption.
      * apply perm_skip. apply IH.
        apply select_rest_length in E. simpl in H. lia.
Qed.
selsort_perm.explanation: By induction on n. For the non-trivial case, use select_perm and select_rest_length to establish the permutation.

selection_sort_perm.solution:
Proof.
  intros. unfold selection_sort.
  apply selsort_perm. reflexivity.
Qed.
selection_sort_perm.explanation: Unfold selection_sort and apply selsort_perm with the length of the list.

select_fst_leq.solution:
Proof.
  induction al as [| h t IH]; intros; simpl in *.
  - inv H. lia.
  - bdestruct (x <=? h).
    + destruct (select x t) as [j l'] eqn:E. inv H.
      apply IH in E. lia.
    + destruct (select h t) as [j l'] eqn:E. inv H.
      apply IH in E. lia.
Qed.
select_fst_leq.explanation: By induction on al. In each branch of the bdestruct, use the IH and lia.

le_all__le_one.solution:
Proof.
  intros. eapply Forall_forall; eauto.
Qed.
le_all__le_one.explanation: Direct application of the Forall_forall lemma from the standard library.

select_smallest.solution:
Proof.
  induction al as [| h t IH]; intros; simpl in *.
  - inv H. constructor.
  - bdestruct (x <=? h).
    + destruct (select x t) as [j l'] eqn:E. inv H.
      constructor.
      * apply select_fst_leq in E. lia.
      * apply IH in E. assumption.
    + destruct (select h t) as [j l'] eqn:E. inv H.
      constructor.
      * apply select_fst_leq in E. lia.
      * apply IH in E. assumption.
Qed.
select_smallest.explanation: By induction on al. Use select_fst_leq to show the selected element is <= each element in the rest.

select_in.solution:
Proof.
  induction al as [| h t IH]; intros; simpl in *.
  - inv H. left. reflexivity.
  - bdestruct (x <=? h).
    + destruct (select x t) as [j l'] eqn:E. inv H.
      specialize (IH x j l' E). simpl in IH.
      destruct IH as [IH | IH].
      * left. assumption.
      * right. right. assumption.
    + destruct (select h t) as [j l'] eqn:E. inv H.
      specialize (IH h j l' E). simpl in IH.
      destruct IH as [IH | IH].
      * right. left. assumption.
      * right. right. assumption.
Qed.
select_in.explanation: By induction on al. Track whether the selected element came from x or from the list.

cons_of_small_maintains_sort.solution:
Proof.
  intros bl y n Hlen Hle Hsorted.
  destruct (selsort bl n) as [| z rest] eqn:E.
  - constructor.
  - constructor.
    + unfold le_all in Hle.
      assert (In z (selsort bl n)).
      { rewrite E. left. reflexivity. }
      apply Permutation_sym in (selsort_perm n bl Hlen).
      apply Permutation_in with (selsort bl n); auto.
      rewrite E. left. reflexivity.
      Restart.
  intros bl y n Hlen Hle Hsorted.
  destruct (selsort bl n) as [| z rest] eqn:E.
  - constructor.
  - constructor.
    + assert (Hin: In z bl).
      { apply Permutation_in with (selsort bl n).
        apply Permutation_sym. apply selsort_perm. assumption.
        rewrite E. left. reflexivity. }
      eapply le_all__le_one; eauto.
    + assumption.
Qed.
cons_of_small_maintains_sort.explanation: Destruct selsort bl n. If empty, trivial. If non-empty with head z, show z is in bl using Permutation, then use le_all to get y <= z.

selsort_sorted.solution:
Proof.
  induction n as [| n' IH]; intros.
  - destruct al; simpl. constructor. constructor.
  - destruct al as [| x r].
    + simpl. constructor.
    + simpl. destruct (select x r) as [y r'] eqn:E.
      apply cons_of_small_maintains_sort.
      * apply select_rest_length in E. simpl in H. lia.
      * apply select_smallest in E. assumption.
      * apply IH. apply select_rest_length in E. simpl in H. lia.
Qed.
selsort_sorted.explanation: By induction on n. Use cons_of_small_maintains_sort with select_smallest and select_rest_length.

selection_sort_sorted.solution:
Proof.
  intros. unfold selection_sort.
  apply selsort_sorted. reflexivity.
Qed.
selection_sort_sorted.explanation: Unfold and apply selsort_sorted with the list length.

selection_sort_is_correct.solution:
Proof.
  unfold is_a_sorting_algorithm. intros.
  split.
  - apply selection_sort_perm.
  - apply selection_sort_sorted.
Qed.
selection_sort_is_correct.explanation: Direct combination of permutation and sortedness proofs.

selsort'_perm.solution:
Proof.
  induction n as [| n' IH]; intros.
  - destruct l; simpl. constructor. discriminate.
  - destruct l as [| x r].
    + simpl. constructor.
    + rewrite selsort'_equation.
      destruct (select x r) as [y r'] eqn:E.
      apply perm_trans with (y :: r').
      * apply select_perm. assumption.
      * apply perm_skip. apply IH.
        apply select_rest_length in E. simpl in H. lia.
Qed.
selsort'_perm.explanation: Same strategy as selsort_perm but using selsort'_equation to unfold the Function-defined selsort'.

cons_of_small_maintains_sort'.solution:
Proof.
  intros bl y Hle Hsorted.
  destruct (selsort' bl) as [| z rest] eqn:E.
  - constructor.
  - constructor.
    + assert (Hin: In z bl).
      { apply Permutation_in with (selsort' bl).
        apply Permutation_sym. apply selsort'_perm with (n := length bl). reflexivity.
        rewrite E. left. reflexivity. }
      eapply le_all__le_one; eauto.
    + assumption.
Qed.
cons_of_small_maintains_sort'.explanation: Same approach as cons_of_small_maintains_sort, using selsort'_perm.

selsort'_sorted.solution:
Proof.
  induction n as [| n' IH]; intros.
  - destruct al; simpl.
    + rewrite selsort'_equation. constructor.
    + discriminate.
  - destruct al as [| x r].
    + rewrite selsort'_equation. constructor.
    + rewrite selsort'_equation.
      destruct (select x r) as [y r'] eqn:E.
      apply cons_of_small_maintains_sort'.
      * apply select_smallest in E. assumption.
      * apply IH. apply select_rest_length in E. simpl in H. lia.
Qed.
selsort'_sorted.explanation: By induction on n, using selsort'_equation and the helper lemmas.

selsort'_is_correct.solution:
Proof.
  unfold is_a_sorting_algorithm. intros.
  split.
  - apply selsort'_perm with (n := length al). reflexivity.
  - apply selsort'_sorted with (n := length al). reflexivity.
Qed.
selsort'_is_correct.explanation: Direct combination of selsort'_perm and selsort'_sorted.

select_contents.solution:
Proof.
  induction al as [| h t IH]; intros; simpl in *.
  - inv H. simpl. reflexivity.
  - bdestruct (x <=? h).
    + destruct (select x t) as [j l'] eqn:E. inv H.
      specialize (IH x j l' E). simpl.
      rewrite union_swap. simpl in IH.
      rewrite <- IH. rewrite union_swap. reflexivity.
    + destruct (select h t) as [j l'] eqn:E. inv H.
      specialize (IH h j l' E). simpl.
      simpl in IH. rewrite <- IH.
      rewrite (union_swap (singleton x) (singleton h)).
      reflexivity.
Qed.
select_contents.explanation: By induction on al, using union_swap to rearrange multiset unions.

selection_sort_contents.solution:
Proof.
  induction n as [| n' IH]; intros.
  - destruct l; simpl. reflexivity. discriminate.
  - destruct l as [| x r]; simpl.
    + reflexivity.
    + unfold selection_sort. simpl.
      destruct (select x r) as [y r'] eqn:E.
      assert (Hsc: union (singleton x) (contents r) = union (singleton y) (contents r')).
      { apply select_contents with (al := r). assumption. }
      rewrite Hsc.
      simpl.
      rewrite <- IH.
      * reflexivity.
      * apply select_rest_length in E. simpl in H. lia.
Qed.
selection_sort_contents.explanation: By induction on n, using select_contents to show that select preserves contents.

sorted_iff_sorted.solution:
Proof.
  split; intros.
  - induction H; auto.
  - induction H; auto.
Qed.
sorted_iff_sorted.explanation: The two sorted predicates (from Selection and Sort) have the same structure, so the proof is straightforward by induction.

selection_sort_correct'.solution:
Proof.
  unfold Multiset.is_a_sorting_algorithm'. intros.
  split.
  - apply selection_sort_contents with (n := length al). reflexivity.
  - apply sorted_iff_sorted. apply selection_sort_sorted.
Qed.
selection_sort_correct'.explanation: Combines selection_sort_contents and sorted_iff_sorted with selection_sort_sorted.
